title: After-Action Report — Auction Shape: Personality-Driven Bidding, Tranched Caps, Snake-Draft Fill, Sellback Trades
scope: This AAR picks up where `aar-live-auction-replay.md` left off — the live page and the guilder rescale were already in. The brief here was different: make the auction *feel right* before shipping, not just render correctly.

rationale.item: The previous PR (`1dbd273`) produced a flat IPL-shape under the guilder rescale: top 10 sales clustered at ƒ54-57 cr, mid-tier 60-OVR clearings at ƒ42-51 cr. A 7-OVR talent gap was only producing a 12% price gap, and Vickrey was collapsing at the per-team cap because every team's max-bid sat near the same ceiling.

"There's no salary floor in this league and the engine should always be looking for arbitrage, rather than having everyone bid all the time" — personalities that randomize per seed so teams pursue consistent strategies, not freewheeling per-lot randomness

delegation_events.decision: Dial in the auction shape before shipping
delegation_events.quote: The user said "dial that in before I shop this, then," and then sent a fast sequence of design notes

delegation_events.decision: Wire manager personalities and organizational strength into the dice rolls
delegation_events.quote: "Wire the manager personalities and the organizational strength into the dice rolls"

delegation_events.decision: Base curve pivot lower than the floor (OVR 20 or 25); raise the cap; flexible per-round/bucketed cap
delegation_events.quote: "The base should actually be lower than the floor, OVR 20 or 25" / "Why not raise the cap?" / "Why not have a flexible cap that can rise — per-round cap? Buckets of rounds?"

delegation_events.decision: Hard-floor minimum-salary deals operate as a snake draft, not an auction
delegation_events.quote: "The hard floor minimum salary deals should just operate like a draft not an auction at all" / "Snake draft, like roster fill I mean"

delegation_events.decision: Steeper aggression on the top end; true difference between top 25 players and everyone else
delegation_events.quote: "Steeper aggression on the top end is a good call. There should be a true difference between the top 25 players in the pool versus everyone else… you'd expect those first 3 picks to be marquee purchases"

delegation_events.decision: Sellback/trade mechanic where the asset is auction budget
delegation_events.quote: "That should lead to an overpay in different round since the money doesn't roll over"

delegation_events.decision: Let the engine drive the numbers, no top-tier target
delegation_events.quote: "I do not care what the top tier number is, let the teams bid and let's see it play out."

delegation_events.decision: Use the new currency system on the live auction page
delegation_events.quote: The user spotted this with "CAN THIE ACUTION USE THE NE CURRENCY I MADE or not?"

actions.description: Quadratic bid curve with lower pivot: replaced linear overall × 5_00_000 with (overall − 25)² × 1_00_000; pure function _bid_base(overall)
Wider noise, discipline-dampened: per-lot noise band uniform(1 − 0.55·(1−d), 1 + 0.50·(1−d))

Manager personality → auction strategy: _team_auction_profile derives discipline, star_bias, aggression from org_strength, mgr_quick_hook, mgr_bullpen_aggression, mgr_joker_aggression; sampled once at start of apply_auction
Tranched per-lot cap: _per_lot_cap with TRANCHE_CAPS (50→0.50, 200→0.20, 500→0.10, None→0.04); cutoffs match live-auction page stage/montage split

Snake-draft for roster fill: top auction_lot_limit (default 500) lots bid; rest fill via snake order at min_bid; drafted lots recorded with winning_bid = min_bid, second_bid = NULL, single rank-1 bid row
Elite bonus on top of the quadratic: base = (overall − 25)² × 1L + max(0, overall − 60)² × 10L
Post-clear sellback trade mechanic with TRADE_THRESHOLD; new helper _team_valuation_noisefree; trade phase uses noise-free valuations, auction keeps noisy _team_bid
Schema: two new nullable columns on auction_results (traded_to_team_id, trade_price) with idempotent ALTER TABLE migration; original Vickrey columns preserved as audit trail

get_live_auction + live page wiring: feed exposes traded_to_abbrev/_name, trade_price, derived final_owner_*; purse rollup nets trade flows; auction_live.html renders ⇄ Traded to line; applyBidsToPurse mirrors server-side rollup

Live page currency wiring: replaced hardcoded $ + toLocaleString with JS fmtMoney() producing o27-money cells, mirroring o27v2/currency.py (format_indian, format_crore, western M/B/K), rates from window.O27_RATES

Cash-drain calibration pass: _team_pressure multiplier (capped 2.5×); variable-cost snake-draft fill max(min_bid, purse_remaining // open_slots); TRADE_LOT_LIMIT = 50 and TRADE_SALES_PER_TEAM = 1 hard caps; valuation-based trade threshold (winner_val × 1.05 instead of winning_bid × 1.05)

risk_posture: Original Vickrey columns (`winner_team_id`, `winning_bid`, `second_bid`, `price`) are preserved as the auction audit trail; the trade is a follow-up event on the same row.
Idempotent ALTER TABLE migration so existing saves attach without manual surgery.
the auction itself still uses the noisy `_team_bid` for the original allocation, preserving the original Vickrey audit trail
Hard floor still leaves `min_bid × remaining_slots` so roster fill always clears.

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end smoke (rng_seed=42, 56-team tiered): marquee top-25 avg ƒ39.5 cr (ƒ29.1–54.0), marquee 26-50 avg ƒ29.5 cr, core avg ƒ22.5 cr, depth avg ƒ14.9 cr, snake-fill flat ƒ50 lakh; top sale Santiago Hargett OVR 68 1B → NYM ƒ54.0 cr
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run the auction with rng_seed=42 on the 56-team tiered config and compare tranche stats

validation_claims.claim: Trades after cash-drain pass: 7 of 500 auction lots (~1.4%), down from unthrottled 41%
validation_claims.check_method: Count rows with traded_to_team_id NOT NULL after a seeded auction run

validation_claims.claim: Cash drain: all 56 teams within ƒ±0 of starting purse
validation_claims.check_method: Sum per-team spend after a seeded auction run

validation_claims.claim: Personality readback: top spenders cluster around small_ball / players_manager / mad_scientist archetypes; bottom spenders cluster around sabermetric_max / iron_manager
validation_claims.check_method: Group auction spend by manager archetype on a seeded run

validation_claims.claim: Top sale moved to ƒ54 cr and the marquee top-25 vs 26-50 gap opened up to 33% (was 18%) after the elite bonus
validation_claims.check_method: Compare tranche averages before/after elite bonus on the same seed

non_actions.item: Unit test pinning _bid_base shape before calibration
non_actions.reason: Skipped writing the test because "the curve is obvious," paid for it twice.

non_actions.item: Surfacing the trade-rate target to the user before shipping TRADE_THRESHOLD = 0.05
non_actions.reason: I had no signal either way and shipped a default. An AskUserQuestion on threshold would have been worth a 30-second interrupt.
BID_QUAD_SCALE, BID_ELITE_SCALE, TRANCHE_CAPS, TRADE_THRESHOLD are all module-level constants ... Should have moved these to config["auction"] from the start; refactor cost is low
Current is single-hop: lot clears, one trade fires, lot is settled.

non_actions.item: Trade visualisation upgrade on live page; /auction static report trade display; auction-format toggle; auction-only personality archetypes; reverse-standings snake order; calibration sweep tooling
non_actions.reason: listed as pointers for follow-up work

residual_risks: The "no rollover ⇒ overpay later" loop didn't actually close ... seller gets cash, but their `_team_bid` formula doesn't reference remaining purse ... net-seller teams exit with ƒ-100 to -325 cr "spend" ... mechanically correct but doesn't match the brief's "overpay" narrative. (Note: §12 cash-drain pass later added _team_pressure addressing this.)
Calibration constants live in Python, not config — a different league has to monkey-patch instead of editing JSON.
Trade visualisation in the live page is muted.
/auction static report doesn't show trades — the data is in the feed; just not surfaced.
Snake-draft order is naive (team_id order, round 2 reversed) instead of reverse-standings.
open_hooks: Tune TRADE_THRESHOLD (try 0.10–0.15); per-lot measurement of 'true' corrections (>20% valuation gap)
Close the 'overpay later' loop via expected_purse pressure factor (code sketch provided)
Iterate the trade phase until no improving trade exists (Pareto-optimise post-Vickrey)
auction.bid_curve sub-dict in 56teams_tiered.json for per-league shapes
auction.format config knob: vickrey | first_price | english
Explicit auction-day Archetype axes (auction_aggression, auction_marquee_focus, auction_patience)
Reverse-standings snake order — trivial change in _advance
30-line calibration sweep script across 5-10 seeds reporting tranche stats
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Auction Shape: Personality-Driven Bidding, Tranched Caps, Snake-Draft Fill, Sellback Trades
1. Quadratic bid curve with lower pivot (cfa32fe)
7. Elite bonus on top of the quadratic (1ea08e5)
11. Live page currency wiring (came in earlier in the session)
12. Cash-drain calibration pass (post-`1ea08e5`, in this commit)
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/new-league-structure-tqk8H; commits cfa32fe (auction shape) and 1ea08e5 (elite tier + sellback). | 'What I'd do differently' retrospective: the off-by-10× bug on BID_QUAD_SCALE (set 10 * 1_00_000 thinking lakh; OVR 63 came out at ƒ144 cr base; took an instrumented per-team bid-debug pass to catch; 5-line unit test would have caught it — assert _bid_base(60) == 12_25_000, assert _bid_base(67) == 1_762_500); dead-end on 'make the cap so low bids can't reach it' — user redirected with 'why not raise the cap?', the per-lot cap had become a hammer when what was needed was a runway; currency-system bypass — prevention is 'grep o27-money' before writing any new money rendering; should have surfaced the trade-rate target (41% trade rate on first ship, borderline noise). | Valuation-threshold rationale: original formulation compared buyer's noise-free val vs winner's noise-inflated bid and stopped firing once the elite-bonus pulled bids above noise-free vals; new formulation says 'trade fires when the wrong team won the lot'; seller's reservation price is their own valuation, not the noisy winning_bid (sunk cost). | Personality variance now shows up in roster shape rather than net spend; every team spends ~ƒ200 cr exactly.
parser_notes: Rich retrospective ('What I'd do differently') recorded mostly in unmapped_content with key items mirrored into non_actions/residual_risks. The 'overpay later' residual risk is partially superseded by §12's _team_pressure fix within the same document.
